title: aegle glutinosa
intro: Aegle glutinosa, also known as the Bael or Bengal quince, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aegle glutinosa include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, as well as having antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Aegle glutinosa can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments.
